summary:
The typical range of connective tissue width around a dental implant is approximately 3-3.65 mm, which aligns with standard anatomical measurements used clinically. This dimension ensures proper integration without compromising adjacent soft-tissue health or aesthetics. \boxed{B}
The summary concisely captures the essential point about the specific width while emphasizing its relevance to both function and patient outcomes.
